fact: May 3 is exactly 123 days into the year - that's like counting 1-2-3!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby birds to hatch - their tiny peeps fill the air as they crack out of their shells!
Dandelions are usually going wild right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere like nature's confetti cannons.
The sun is climbing higher each day, making shadows shorter and giving you about 14 hours of daylight in most northern places.
Taurus season is in full swing - people born around now are supposedly as stubborn as bulls but also love snacks and cozy blankets.
In Australia and other southern places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red before falling.
Bees are typically having their busiest party season - they're drunk on nectar and covered in yellow pollen dust like tiny flying donuts.
The moon phase around May 3 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, the night sky becomes a perfect black canvas for seeing shooting stars.
Spring thunderstorms often rumble through around this time in temperate regions, creating those weird green-tinted skies that make everything look like an alien movie.
Earthworms are usually having a field day in the soft, warm soil - they're basically throwing underground dance parties and making dirt super healthy.
May 3 sits perfectly between the spring equinox and summer solstice, making it the sweet spot when nature can't decide if it wants to be gentle or wild.
